Table of contents

  • 1 Preparation
    • 1.1 Action 1
    • 1.2 Action 2
  • 2 Implementation
    • 2.1 Virologic Failure, Complete-Case Analysis
    • 2.2 Virologic Failure, with Multiple Imputation
    • 2.3 Major Regimen Change, Complete-Case Analysis
    • 2.4 Major Regimen Change, with Multiple Imputation

Protocol for Implementing Distributed Cox Regression on CCASAnet

for Chile (lead site)

Author

Kaixing Liu

Published

August 8, 2025

Modified

August 13, 2025

1 Preparation

1.1 Action 1

  1. Create/Choose a directory in your local machine at your discretion.
    • For example, I created a local directory with absolute path, "/Users/lkx220/Desktop/chile".
  2. Download from the cloud (e.g., OneDrive) the following items,
    • R scripts, distributed-analysis-ccasanet.R and authentic-distributed-analysis.R, to the local directory specified in 1., say "/Users/lkx220/Desktop/chile";
    • site-specific individual-level data: Chile shall download chile_all_trt.rds and chile_all_vl.rds to the same local directory;
    • global set-up file: setup.rds to the same local directory;
    • site-specific instruction: Chile should download protocol-CCASAnet-Chile.html to anywhere in your local machine (this file is meant to guide you the procedures of implementation of this distributed analysis);

1.1.1 Check

Make sure that R scripts, set-up file, and the data are in the same local directory, shown below.

This local directory (chosen at your discretion) would be the working directory in R later.

1.2 Action 2

  1. Open RStudio or R and set working directory as the local directory you chosen in Preparation.

    You may as well run the command below in your R console.

    # setwd("your-working-directory")
setwd("/Users/lkx220/Desktop/chile") # my example

  2. Source the code and import data and set-up file.

    Please execute code below in console.

    source("distributed-analysis-ccasanet.R")
source("authentic-distributed-analysis.R")
chile.vl = readRDS("chile_all_vl.rds")
chile.trt = readRDS("chile_all_trt.rds")
setup = readRDS("setup.rds")
# prepare data for different scenarios
# for Chile
## you can just ignore warning messages
chile.vl.cc = chile.vl %>% subset(!is.na(sex_mode) & !is.na(cd4_v_initial_sqrt) & !is.na(rna_v_initial_log))
chile.vl.mi = mice.local.merge(mice(chile.vl %>% select(all_of(setup[["vl.imp.var"]])), m=setup[["no.of.imputation"]], method = "pmm",
                                 seed = setup[["seed"]], printFlag = FALSE), m=setup[["no.of.imputation"]])
chile.trt.cc = chile.trt %>% subset(!is.na(sex_mode) & !is.na(cd4_v_initial_sqrt) & !is.na(rna_v_initial_log))
chile.trt.mi = mice.local.merge(mice(chile.trt %>% select(all_of(setup[["trt.imp.var"]])), m=setup[["no.of.imputation"]], method = "pmm",
                                 seed = setup[["seed"]], printFlag = FALSE), m=setup[["no.of.imputation"]])

2 Implementation

2.1 Virologic Failure, Complete-Case Analysis

  • Initialization
  • Derivation
  • Estimation
  • Results

  1. Chile runs the command below.

    After running this, there will be a control.json file in the newly generated directory, "vl/complete-case" in the chosen local directory.

    control <- list(project_name = 'virologic_failure_distributed_survival_analysis_ccasanet',
            step = 'initialize',
            sites = c("chile", "brazil", "mexico"),
            heterogeneity = TRUE,
            model = 'ODAC',
            family = 'cox',
            outcome = "Surv(vlfailure_status.years, vlfailure_status==\"virologic failure\")",
            variables = c('sex_mode', 'age', 'clinicalAIDS', 'cd4_v_initial_sqrt', 'rna_v_initial_log', 'calendaryear', 'initialARTtype'),
            xlev = list(sex_mode=c('Female','Male-MSM', "Male-Other"), clinicalAIDS=c('Not clinical AIDS','Clinical AIDS'), initialARTtype=c('NNRTI','PI','INSTI')),
            optim_maxit = 10000,
            init_method = "meta",
            optim_method = "Nelder-Mead",
            lead_site = "chile",
            upload_date = as.character(Sys.time()))
set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.setup(leadsite = "chile", control, dir = getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "vl")

    CHECK

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./vl/complete-case" should be “initialize” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  2. Chile shall upload the control.json file in "./vl/complete-case" to the "./vl/complete-case" directory in the cloud.

    —Wait for Brazil and Mexico to upload their _initialize.json file to the cloud.—

  3. After downloading brazil_initialize.json and mexico_initialize.json in the "./vl/complete-case" directory in OneDrive to local "./vl/complete-case" directory,

    CHECK

    Then run the command below. The same control.json file will be updated automatically.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.local(chile.vl.cc, "chile", getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "vl")

    CHECK

    and

    The “step” in control.json file in "./vl/complete-case" should be “derive” now (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  4. Chile uploads the updated control.json file in "./vl/complete-case" in local machine to "./vl/complete-case" in OneDrive.

—Wait for Brazil and Mexico to upload their _derive.json file.—

  1. After downloading brazil_derive.json and mexico_derive.json from "./vl/complete-case" in the cloud to local "./vl/complete-case",

    CHECK

    Then run the command below.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.derive(chile.vl.cc, "chile", getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "vl")

    CHECK (chile_derive.json)

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./vl/complete-case" should be “estimate” now (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  1. Chile runs this.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.estimate(chile.vl.cc, "chile", getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "vl")

    CHECK (chile_estimate.json)

  1. Chile runs this.
vl.dist.cc = pda.summary("chile", c("brazil", "mexico"), getwd(), 1, complete = TRUE, outcome="vl")
saveRDS(vl.dist.cc, file = "vl_dist_cc.rds")

vl.meta.cc = meta.mi(dist.meta(c("chile", "brazil", "mexico"), getwd(), 1, complete = TRUE, outcome="vl"))
saveRDS(vl.meta.cc, file = "vl_meta_cc.rds")

  1. CHECK and upload vl_dist_cc.rds and vl_meta_cc.rds in the local directory to parental OneDrive folder.

—You are Done for federated analysis in this scenario.—

Now you probably get familiar with the procedure.

For virologic failure with multiple imputation later, the procedures are the same overall except that we will do this per imputed data set.

No worries. The command given below realize this in parallel and this means you still just need run it once.

But you need do more when downloading and uploading files since we have multiple imputed data sets.

2.2 Virologic Failure, with Multiple Imputation

  • Initialization
  • Derivation
  • Estimation
  • Results

  1. Chile runs the command below.

    control <- list(project_name = 'virologic_failure_distributed_survival_analysis_ccasanet',
            step = 'initialize',
            sites = c("chile", "brazil", "mexico"),
            heterogeneity = TRUE,
            model = 'ODAC',
            family = 'cox',
            outcome = "Surv(vlfailure_status.years, vlfailure_status==\"virologic failure\")",
            variables = c('sex_mode', 'age', 'clinicalAIDS', 'cd4_v_initial_sqrt', 'rna_v_initial_log', 'calendaryear', 'initialARTtype'),
            xlev = list(sex_mode=c('Female','Male-MSM', "Male-Other"), clinicalAIDS=c('Not clinical AIDS','Clinical AIDS'), initialARTtype=c('NNRTI','PI','INSTI')),
            optim_maxit = 10000,
            init_method = "meta",
            optim_method = "Nelder-Mead",
            lead_site = "chile",
            upload_date = as.character(Sys.time()))
set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.setup(leadsite = "chile", control, dir = getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome = "vl")

    CHECK (newly generated directories indexed by number 1-10 in ./vl and control.json file within each number-indexed directory)

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./vl/1" should be “initialize” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  2. Chile uploads each control.json file in the number-indexed directory to corresponding number-indexed directory in OneDrive.

    —Wait for Brazil and Mexico to obtain and upload their _initialize.json file.—

  3. After downloading each pair of brazil_initialize.json and mexico_initialize.json in the number-indexed directory in OneDrive to corresponding local number-indexed directory,

    CHECK

    Run the command below. The same control.json file will be updated automatically.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.local(chile.vl.mi, "chile", getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome = "vl")

    CHECK (chile_initialize.json)

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./vl/1" should be “derive” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  4. Chile updates each control.json file in the number-indexed directory to corresponding number-indexed directory in OneDrive.

—Wait for Brazil and Mexico to upload their _derive.json file.—

  1. After downloading each pair of brazil_derive.json and mexico_derive.json in number-indexed directories in OneDrive to corresponding local number-indexed directories,

    CHECK

    Then run the command below.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.derive(chile.vl.mi, "chile", getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome="vl")

    CHECK

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./vl/1" should be “estimate” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  1. Chile runs this.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.estimate(chile.vl.mi, "chile", getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome="vl")
  1. Chile runs this.
vl.dist.mi = pda.summary("chile", c("brazil", "mexico"), getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome="vl")
saveRDS(vl.dist.mi, file = "vl_dist_mi.rds")

vl.meta.mi = meta.mi(dist.meta(c("chile", "brazil", "mexico"), getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome="vl"))
saveRDS(vl.meta.mi, file = "vl_meta_mi.rds")
  1. CHECK and upload vl_dist_mi.rds and vl_meta_mi.rds in the local directory to parental OneDrive folder.

—You are Done for federated analysis in this scenario.—

Regarding Major Regimen Change, we just repeat the above procedures.

2.3 Major Regimen Change, Complete-Case Analysis

  • Initialization
  • Derivation
  • Estimation
  • Results

  1. Chile runs the command below.

    After running this, there will be a control.json file in the newly generated directory, "trt/complete-case" in the chosen local directory.

    control <- list(project_name = 'major_regimen_change_distributed_survival_analysis_ccasanet',
                step = 'initialize',
                sites = c("chile", "brazil", "mexico"),
                heterogeneity = TRUE,
                model = 'ODAC',
                family = 'cox',
                outcome = "Surv(ARTchange_status.years, ARTchange_status==\"major treatment change\")",
                variables = c('sex_mode', 'age', 'clinicalAIDS', 'cd4_v_initial_sqrt', 'rna_v_initial_log', 'calendaryear', 'initialARTtype'),
                xlev = list(sex_mode=c('Female','Male-MSM', 'Male-Other'),  clinicalAIDS=c('Not clinical AIDS','Clinical AIDS'), initialARTtype=c('NNRTI','PI','INSTI')),
                optim_maxit = 10000,
                init_method = "meta",
                optim_method = "Nelder-Mead", 
                lead_site = "chile",
                upload_date = as.character(Sys.time()))
set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.setup(leadsite = "chile", control, dir = getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "trt")

    CHECK similarly

  2. Chile shall upload the control.json file in "./trt/complete-case" to the "./trt/complete-case" directory in the cloud.

    —Wait for Brazil and Mexico to upload their _initialize.json file to the cloud.—

  3. After downloading brazil_initialize.json and mexico_initialize.json in the "./trt/complete-case" directory in OneDrive to local "./trt/complete-case" directory,

    CHECK similarly as the case for virologic failure

    Then run the command below. The same control.json file will be updated automatically.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.local(chile.trt.cc, "chile", getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "trt")

    CHECK similarly

    and

    The “step” in control.json file in "./trt/complete-case" should be “derive” now (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  4. Chile uploads the updated control.json file in "./trt/complete-case" in local machine to "./trt/complete-case" in OneDrive.

—Wait for Brazil and Mexico to upload their _derive.json file.—

  1. After downloading brazil_derive.json and mexico_derive.json from "./trt/complete-case" in the cloud to local "./trt/complete-case",

    CHECK similarly as the case for virologic failure

    Then run the command below.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.derive(chile.trt.cc, "chile", getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "trt")

    CHECK similarly (chile_derive.json)

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./trt/complete-case" should be “estimate” now (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  1. Chile runs this.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.estimate(chile.trt.cc, "chile", getwd(), m = 1, complete = TRUE, outcome = "trt")

    CHECK similarly (chile_estimate.json)

  1. Chile runs this.
trt.dist.cc = pda.summary("chile", c("brazil", "mexico"), getwd(), 1, complete = TRUE, outcome="trt")
saveRDS(trt.dist.cc, file = "trt_dist_cc.rds")

trt.meta.cc = meta.mi(dist.meta(c("chile", "brazil", "mexico"), getwd(), 1, complete = TRUE, outcome="trt"))
saveRDS(trt.meta.cc, file = "trt_meta_cc.rds")

  1. CHECK and upload trt_dist_cc.rds and trt_meta_cc.rds in the local directory to parental OneDrive folder.

—You are Done for federated analysis in this scenario.—

Now you probably get familiar with the procedure.

For major regimen change with multiple imputation later, the procedures are the same overall except that we will do this per imputed data set.

No worries. The command given below realize this in parallel and this means you still just need run it once.

But you need do more when downloading and uploading files since we have multiple imputed data sets.

2.4 Major Regimen Change, with Multiple Imputation

  • Initialization
  • Derivation
  • Estimation
  • Results

  1. Chile runs the command below.

    control <- list(project_name = 'major_regimen_change_distributed_survival_analysis_ccasanet',
                step = 'initialize',
                sites = c("chile", "brazil", "mexico"),
                heterogeneity = TRUE,
                model = 'ODAC',
                family = 'cox',
                outcome = "Surv(ARTchange_status.years, ARTchange_status==\"major treatment change\")",
                variables = c('sex_mode', 'age', 'clinicalAIDS', 'cd4_v_initial_sqrt', 'rna_v_initial_log', 'calendaryear', 'initialARTtype'),
                xlev = list(sex_mode=c('Female','Male-MSM', 'Male-Other'),  clinicalAIDS=c('Not clinical AIDS','Clinical AIDS'), initialARTtype=c('NNRTI','PI','INSTI')),
                optim_maxit = 10000,
                init_method = "meta",
                optim_method = "Nelder-Mead", 
                lead_site = "chile",
                upload_date = as.character(Sys.time()))
set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.setup(leadsite = "chile", control, dir = getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome = "trt")

    CHECK similarly (newly generated directories indexed by number 1-10 in ./vl and control.json file within each number-indexed directory)

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./trt/1" should be “initialize” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  2. Chile uploads each control.json file in the number-indexed directory to corresponding number-indexed directory in OneDrive.

    —Wait for Brazil and Mexico to obtain and upload their _initialize.json file.—

  3. After downloading each pair of brazil_initialize.json and mexico_initialize.json in the number-indexed directory in OneDrive to corresponding local number-indexed directory,

    CHECK similarly

    Run the command below. The same control.json file will be updated automatically.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.local(chile.trt.mi, "chile", getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome = "trt")

    CHECK similarly (chile_initialize.json)

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./trt/1" should be “derive” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  4. Chile updates each control.json file in the number-indexed directory to corresponding number-indexed directory in OneDrive.

—Wait for Brazil and Mexico to upload their _derive.json file.—

  1. After downloading each pair of brazil_derive.json and mexico_derive.json in number-indexed directories in OneDrive to corresponding local number-indexed directories,

    CHECK similarly

    Then run the command below.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.derive(chile.trt.mi, "chile", getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome="trt")

    CHECK similarly

    and

    the “step” in control.json file in "./trt/1" should be “estimate” (you can just open the control.json file or preview it if possible).

  1. Chile runs this.

    set.seed(setup[["seed"]])
pda.estimate(chile.trt.mi, "chile", getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome="trt")
  1. Chile runs this.
trt.dist.mi = pda.summary("chile", c("brazil", "mexico"), getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome="trt")
saveRDS(trt.dist.mi, file = "trt_dist_mi.rds")

trt.meta.mi = meta.mi(dist.meta(c("chile", "brazil", "mexico"), getwd(), m = setup[["no.of.imputation"]], outcome="trt"))
saveRDS(trt.meta.mi, file = "trt_meta_mi.rds")
  1. CHECK and upload trt_dist_mi.rds and trt_meta_mi.rds in the local directory to parental OneDrive folder.

—You are Done for federated analysis in this scenario.—
